title: Letta
oneLiner: Platform for stateful agents with advanced memory, personalization, and learning over time.
summary: Letta is an open-source platform for building stateful agents that remember users, maintain memory blocks, and interact through APIs and SDKs.
whyItMatters: Letta matters because memory is not just a database problem; it changes how an agent behaves over time. Letta gives builders a more structured model for stateful agents and persistent memory.
bestFor: Teams that need memory APIs and SDKs instead of ad hoc context prompts
Researchers exploring learning and self-improving agents
notFor: Users who want a fully managed consumer product with no setup work
Teams that cannot review the linked source, license, and operational requirements before adoption
seoTitle: Letta: Memory Systems resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: Letta profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Platform for stateful agents with advanced memory, personalization, and learning over time.

coreStrengths.title: Stateful agent model
coreStrengths.description: Letta treats memory as part of the agent state rather than a loose prompt append.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: That makes it easier to build agents that remain coherent across sessions.

coreStrengths.title: API and SDK surface
coreStrengths.description: Letta provides APIs and client SDKs for integrating stateful agents into applications.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Product teams need stable interfaces, not just demos.

coreStrengths.title: Active open-source platform
coreStrengths.description: The project has frequent releases and documentation around agents and memory.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Active maintenance matters for infrastructure that sits inside an application.

useCaseNotes.title: Personalized AI agents
useCaseNotes.description: Build assistants that retain a persona and user-specific memory blocks.

useCaseNotes.title: Stateful agent APIs
useCaseNotes.description: Expose memory-backed agents inside applications through a service interface.

useCaseNotes.title: Learning workflows
useCaseNotes.description: Experiment with agents that improve from ongoing interactions.

compareNotes.title: Choose Letta for stateful agent architecture
compareNotes.summary: Letta is broader than memory storage; it is closer to a platform for agents whose behavior depends on state.

commandLine.label: Clone Letta

faq.question: Is Letta open source?
faq.answer: Letta is listed with Apache-2.0 based on its official source links. Always re-check the repository or model card before production use.
